subject: Gaming
title: Papers, Please: Empathy Through Bureaucracy
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Papers, Please: Empathy Through Bureaucracy.

hook.text: A game about checking passports at a border. It sounds boring. It's one of gaming's most emotional experiences.

buildup.text: Lucas Pope created Papers, Please as a statement about immigration, authority, and moral compromise.

discovery.text: You play as a border inspector in a fictional communist country. Each stamp carries moral weight.

twist.text: Denying entry feeds your family. Showing mercy costs your paycheck. Every decision has human consequences.

climax.text: Papers, Please proved that mundane mechanics can create profound emotional and political experiences.

punchline.text: A passport stamp. A moral dilemma. Bureaucracy became art.

quiz.question: What makes Papers, Please emotionally powerful?
options: Every bureaucratic decision carries real moral and human consequences
It has the best graphics of any indie game
